message:
Add Astro documentation site with Starlight and GitHub Pages deploy

- 7 doc pages: overview, getting started, architecture, REST API,
  git protocol, WASM engine, deployment
- Starlight theme with sidebar navigation and search
- GitHub Pages workflow triggered on docs/** changes

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+name: Deploy Docs
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+    paths: [docs/**]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+permissions:
+  pages: write
+  id-token: write
+
+concurrency:
+  group: pages
+  cancel-in-progress: false
+
+jobs:
+  deploy:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    environment: github-pages
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: 22
+          cache: pnpm
+
+      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
+
+      - run: cd docs && npm ci
+
+      - run: cd docs && npx astro build
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
+        with:
+          path: docs/dist
+
+      - uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+---
+title: Architecture
+description: How gitmode works under the hood.
+---
+
+## Overview
+
+![Architecture](/gitmode/architecture.svg)
+
+gitmode runs entirely on Cloudflare's edge infrastructure with three components:
+
+| Component | Role |
+|-----------|------|
+| **Worker** | HTTP router — matches git protocol and REST API routes, forwards to Durable Object |
+| **RepoStore DO** | Per-repo Durable Object with embedded SQLite for refs, HEAD, metadata, commit index |
+| **R2** | Object storage for git objects (blobs, trees, commits, tags) as zlib-compressed loose objects |
+
+## Request Flow
+
+1. **Git client** sends HTTP request (e.g., `git push`)
+2. **Worker** matches the URL pattern, extracts owner/repo, gets the RepoStore DO handle
+3. **Worker** forwards the request to the DO with action headers (`x-action`, `x-repo-path`)
+4. **RepoStore DO** handles the action:
+   - For git protocol: delegates to `handleInfoRefs`, `handleUploadPack`, or `handleReceivePack`
+   - For REST API: delegates to `GitPorcelain` methods via `handleApiAction`
+5. **GitEngine** reads/writes objects from R2 and refs from SQLite
+6. **Zig WASM** handles binary operations: SHA-1 hashing, zlib, packfile parsing, delta encoding
+
+## Data Model
+
+### SQLite Tables (per DO)
+
+```sql
+-- Branch and tag refs
+CREATE TABLE refs (
+  name TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- e.g. "heads/main", "tags/v1.0"
+  sha TEXT NOT NULL
+);
+
+-- HEAD pointer (symbolic ref or detached SHA)
+CREATE TABLE head (
+  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
+  value TEXT NOT NULL     -- e.g. "ref: refs/heads/main" or a raw SHA
+);
+
+-- Repository metadata
+CREATE TABLE repo_meta (
+  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
+  owner TEXT NOT NULL,
+  name TEXT NOT NULL,
+  description TEXT DEFAULT '',
+  visibility TEXT DEFAULT 'public',
+  default_branch TEXT DEFAULT 'main',
+  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
+  updated_at TEXT
+);
+
+-- Commit index for fast log queries
+CREATE TABLE commits (
+  sha TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+  author TEXT NOT NULL,
+  message TEXT NOT NULL,
+  timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL
+);
+```
+
+### R2 Object Layout
+
+Git objects are stored at `{owner}/{repo}/{sha}` as zlib-compressed data with a type prefix byte:
+
+| Type | Value | Description |
+|------|-------|-------------|
+| blob | 1 | File content |
+| tree | 2 | Directory listing (mode + name + SHA entries) |
+| commit | 3 | Commit metadata (tree, parents, author, message) |
+| tag | 4 | Annotated tag (object, type, tag name, tagger, message) |
+
+## Consistency Model
+
+Each repository maps to exactly one Durable Object instance. All ref reads and writes go through the DO's SQLite, which provides:
+
+- **Strong consistency** — reads always see the latest writes
+- **Serialized updates** — concurrent pushes are serialized by the DO
+- **Atomic multi-ref updates** — a single push can update multiple refs atomically
+
+R2 objects are immutable (content-addressed by SHA-1), so they don't require coordination.

@@ -0,0 +1,125 @@
+---
+title: Deployment
+description: Deploy gitmode to Cloudflare Workers.
+---
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+- Cloudflare account with Workers Paid plan (for Durable Objects)
+- R2 bucket for git object storage
+- Wrangler CLI (`pnpm add -g wrangler`)
+
+## Wrangler Configuration
+
+Your `wrangler.jsonc` should include:
+
+```jsonc
+{
+  "name": "gitmode",
+  "main": "src/worker.ts",
+  "compatibility_date": "2025-01-01",
+  "r2_buckets": [
+    {
+      "binding": "OBJECTS",
+      "bucket_name": "gitmode-objects"
+    }
+  ],
+  "durable_objects": {
+    "bindings": [
+      {
+        "name": "REPO_STORE",
+        "class_name": "RepoStore"
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "migrations": [
+    {
+      "tag": "v1",
+      "new_sqlite_classes": ["RepoStore"]
+    }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+## Deploy
+
+```bash
+# Build WASM (if not already built)
+pnpm run build:wasm
+
+# Deploy to Cloudflare
+pnpm run deploy
+```
+
+## Create R2 Bucket
+
+If you haven't created the R2 bucket yet:
+
+```bash
+wrangler r2 bucket create gitmode-objects
+```
+
+## Verify Deployment
+
+After deploying, verify the server is running:
+
+```bash
+# Should return "gitmode"
+curl https://your-worker.your-subdomain.workers.dev/
+
+# Initialize a repo
+curl -X POST https://your-worker.your-subdomain.workers.dev/api/repos/myuser/myrepo/init
+
+# Clone it
+git clone https://your-worker.your-subdomain.workers.dev/myuser/myrepo.git
+```
+
+## Custom Domain
+
+To use a custom domain, add a route in your Wrangler config or use the Cloudflare dashboard:
+
+```jsonc
+{
+  "routes": [
+    { "pattern": "git.example.com/*", "zone_name": "example.com" }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+## Limits
+
+| Resource | Limit |
+|----------|-------|
+| Worker request size | 100MB (Workers Paid) |
+| R2 object size | 5TB per object |
+| Durable Object storage | 10GB SQLite per DO |
+| Durable Object CPU | 30s per request |
+| R2 operations | 10M Class A / 100M Class B per month (free tier) |
+
+For typical git repositories, these limits are more than sufficient. Large binary files (LFS) are stored as regular git objects in R2.
+
+## GitHub Actions
+
+To auto-deploy on push:
+
+```yaml
+name: Deploy
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+    paths-ignore: [docs/**]
+jobs:
+  deploy:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: 22
+          cache: pnpm
+      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
+      - run: npx wrangler deploy
+        env:
+          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
+```

@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+---
+title: Getting Started
+description: Set up and deploy gitmode.
+---
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) 22+
+- [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/)
+- [Zig](https://ziglang.org/) 0.15+ (for WASM builds)
+- [Binaryen](https://github.com/WebAssembly/binaryen) (`wasm-opt`, `wasm-metadce`)
+- A [Cloudflare](https://cloudflare.com) account with Workers, R2, and Durable Objects
+
+## Setup
+
+```bash
+git clone https://github.com/teamchong/gitmode.git
+cd gitmode
+pnpm install
+```
+
+## Build WASM
+
+The Zig WASM module provides SHA-1, zlib, packfile parsing, and delta encoding:
+
+```bash
+pnpm run build:wasm
+```
+
+This compiles the Zig code, runs `wasm-metadce` to remove unused exports, and optimizes with `wasm-opt -Oz`.
+
+## Development
+
+Start a local dev server with Wrangler:
+
+```bash
+pnpm run dev
+```
+
+Then use git against the local server:
+
+```bash
+# Initialize a repo via REST API
+curl -X POST http://localhost:8787/api/repos/myuser/myrepo/init
+
+# Clone (will be empty initially)
+git clone http://localhost:8787/myuser/myrepo.git
+
+# Or commit files via REST API
+curl -X POST http://localhost:8787/api/repos/myuser/myrepo/commits \
+  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
+  -d '{
+    "ref": "main",
+    "message": "initial commit",
+    "author": "Your Name",
+    "email": "you@example.com",
+    "files": [{"path": "README.md", "content": "# My Repo"}]
+  }'
+```
+
+## Testing
+
+```bash
+# Unit + integration tests (vitest with miniflare)
+pnpm test
+
+# Conformance tests with real git CLI
+pnpm run test:conformance
+```
+
+## Deploy
+
+```bash
+pnpm run deploy
+```
+
+Ensure your `wrangler.jsonc` has the R2 bucket and Durable Object bindings configured.

@@ -0,0 +1,112 @@
+---
+title: Git Protocol
+description: Smart HTTP git transport implementation.
+---
+
+gitmode implements the [Git Smart HTTP protocol](https://git-scm.com/docs/http-protocol), enabling standard git clients to clone, fetch, and push.
+
+## Endpoints
+
+| Method | Path | Description |
+|--------|------|-------------|
+| `GET` | `/:owner/:repo.git/info/refs?service=git-upload-pack` | Ref advertisement for fetch/clone |
+| `GET` | `/:owner/:repo.git/info/refs?service=git-receive-pack` | Ref advertisement for push |
+| `POST` | `/:owner/:repo.git/git-upload-pack` | Pack negotiation and download |
+| `POST` | `/:owner/:repo.git/git-receive-pack` | Pack upload and ref update |
+| `GET` | `/:owner/:repo.git/HEAD` | Read HEAD ref |
+
+## Capabilities
+
+gitmode advertises these capabilities during ref advertisement:
+
+| Capability | Description |
+|------------|-------------|
+| `report-status` | Server reports per-ref update status |
+| `delete-refs` | Server accepts ref deletion (`git push --delete`) |
+| `multi_ack_detailed` | Multi-ack protocol for efficient fetch |
+| `side-band-64k` | Multiplexed data/progress/error channels |
+| `thin-pack` | Accept thin packs (base objects may be missing from pack) |
+| `ofs-delta` | Offset-based delta encoding |
+| `agent=gitmode/1.0` | Server identification |
+| `object-format=sha1` | SHA-1 object hashing |
+| `symref=HEAD:refs/heads/main` | Dynamic — reflects actual HEAD target |
+
+## Usage Examples
+
+### Clone
+
+```bash
+git clone http://localhost:8787/owner/repo.git
+```
+
+### Push
+
+```bash
+cd repo
+echo "hello" > file.txt
+git add file.txt
+git commit -m "add file"
+git push
+```
+
+### Branch Operations
+
+```bash
+# Create and push a branch
+git checkout -b feature
+git push -u origin feature
+
+# Delete a remote branch
+git push origin --delete feature
+```
+
+### Tags
+
+```bash
+# Lightweight tag
+git tag v1.0
+git push origin v1.0
+
+# Annotated tag
+git tag -a v2.0 -m "Release v2.0"
+git push --tags
+```
+
+## Protocol Flow
+
+### Clone/Fetch
+
+1. Client sends `GET /info/refs?service=git-upload-pack`
+2. Server returns ref list with capabilities
+3. Client sends `POST /git-upload-pack` with "want" lines (SHAs to fetch) and "have" lines (SHAs already held)
+4. Server builds a packfile containing requested objects, wrapped in sideband-64k framing
+5. Client unpacks and updates local refs
+
+### Push
+
+1. Client sends `GET /info/refs?service=git-receive-pack`
+2. Server returns ref list with capabilities
+3. Client sends `POST /git-receive-pack` with ref update commands and a packfile
+4. Server parses the packfile, stores objects in R2, updates refs in SQLite
+5. Server returns per-ref status report
+
+## Packfile Format
+
+The Zig WASM engine handles all packfile operations:
+
+- **Pack header parsing** — validates magic bytes (`PACK`), version (2), and object count
+- **Entry parsing** — reads variable-length type/size headers, decompresses with zlib
+- **Delta resolution** — applies OFS_DELTA and REF_DELTA instructions to reconstruct objects
+- **Pack building** — creates packfiles from a set of objects for upload-pack responses
+
+## Sideband Protocol
+
+Data from the server is multiplexed into channels:
+
+| Channel | Byte | Purpose |
+|---------|------|---------|
+| Pack data | `0x01` | Packfile bytes |
+| Progress | `0x02` | Human-readable progress messages |
+| Error | `0x03` | Error messages |
+
+Each sideband message is wrapped in a pkt-line (4-byte hex length prefix). Maximum payload per pkt-line is 65515 bytes (65520 total minus 4 header minus 1 channel byte).

@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+title: gitmode
+description: Serverless git hosting on Cloudflare Workers with Zig WASM engine.
+template: splash
+hero:
+  tagline: Full git server on Cloudflare Workers. Push, clone, branch, merge — all serverless with zero infrastructure.
+  actions:
+    - text: Get Started
+      link: /gitmode/getting-started/
+      icon: right-arrow
+      variant: primary
+    - text: REST API
+      link: /gitmode/rest-api/
+      variant: minimal
+---
+
+import { Card, CardGrid } from "@astrojs/starlight/components";
+
+## Features
+
+<CardGrid>
+  <Card title="Full Git Protocol" icon="pencil">
+    Smart HTTP transport with info-refs, upload-pack, and receive-pack. Works with standard `git clone`, `git push`, `git fetch`.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="REST API" icon="setting">
+    30+ endpoints for programmatic access. Init repos, commit files, create branches, merge, cherry-pick, revert — all via JSON.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="Zig WASM Engine" icon="rocket">
+    SHA-1 hashing, zlib compression, packfile parsing, delta encoding — all in Zig compiled to WASM with SIMD acceleration.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="Durable Objects + R2" icon="puzzle">
+    Each repo is a Durable Object with embedded SQLite for refs and metadata. Git objects stored in R2 as compressed loose objects.
+  </Card>
+</CardGrid>
+
+## How It Works
+
+```
+Git Client  ──HTTP──▶  Cloudflare Worker  ──▶  RepoStore DO (SQLite)
+                                           ──▶  R2 (git objects)
+                                           ──▶  Zig WASM (SHA-1, zlib, packfiles)
+```
+
+Every repository maps to a single Durable Object instance. The DO's embedded SQLite stores refs (branches, tags, HEAD) and metadata. Git objects (blobs, trees, commits, tags) live in R2 as zlib-compressed loose objects. The Zig WASM module handles all binary operations: SHA-1 hashing, zlib inflate/deflate, packfile parsing, and delta encoding.

@@ -0,0 +1,351 @@
+---
+title: REST API
+description: Programmatic API for all git operations.
+---
+
+All REST API endpoints use the base path `/api/repos/:owner/:repo`. Request and response bodies are JSON.
+
+## Repository
+
+### Initialize
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/init
+```
+
+```json
+{ "defaultBranch": "main" }
+```
+
+Creates the repository with an empty HEAD pointing to the specified branch.
+
+## Files
+
+### Read File
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/files?ref=main&path=src/index.ts
+```
+
+Returns the file content at the given ref and path:
+
+```json
+{ "content": "console.log('hi')", "size": 17 }
+```
+
+### List Files
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/files?ref=main
+```
+
+Lists entries at the root tree:
+
+```json
+{
+  "files": [
+    { "path": "README.md", "mode": "100644", "type": "blob", "sha": "abc..." },
+    { "path": "src", "mode": "40000", "type": "tree", "sha": "def..." }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### List All Files
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/files/all?ref=main
+```
+
+Recursively lists all files (blobs only):
+
+```json
+{
+  "files": [
+    { "path": "README.md", "mode": "100644", "type": "blob", "sha": "abc..." },
+    { "path": "src/index.ts", "mode": "100644", "type": "blob", "sha": "ghi..." }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+## Commits
+
+### Create Commit
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/commits
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "ref": "main",
+  "message": "Add feature",
+  "author": "Alice",
+  "email": "alice@example.com",
+  "files": [
+    { "path": "feature.ts", "content": "export const x = 1;" },
+    { "path": "old-file.ts", "content": null }
+  ],
+  "timestamp": 1700000000
+}
+```
+
+Set `content` to `null` to delete a file. Returns `{ "sha": "abc..." }`.
+
+### Log
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/log?ref=main&max=50
+```
+
+Returns commit history walking from the given ref:
+
+```json
+{
+  "commits": [
+    {
+      "sha": "abc...",
+      "tree": "def...",
+      "parents": ["ghi..."],
+      "author": "Alice",
+      "authorEmail": "alice@example.com",
+      "authorTimestamp": 1700000000,
+      "committer": "Alice",
+      "committerEmail": "alice@example.com",
+      "committerTimestamp": 1700000000,
+      "message": "Add feature\n"
+    }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+## Branches
+
+### List Branches
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/branches
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "branches": [
+    { "name": "main", "sha": "abc...", "isHead": true },
+    { "name": "feature", "sha": "def...", "isHead": false }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### Create Branch
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/branches
+```
+
+```json
+{ "name": "feature", "startPoint": "main" }
+```
+
+`startPoint` defaults to HEAD if omitted.
+
+### Delete Branch
+
+```http
+DELETE /api/repos/:owner/:repo/branches/:name
+```
+
+Cannot delete the currently checked-out branch.
+
+### Rename Branch
+
+```http
+PATCH /api/repos/:owner/:repo/branches/:name
+```
+
+```json
+{ "newName": "feature-v2" }
+```
+
+Updates HEAD if it pointed to the old branch.
+
+## Checkout
+
+### Switch Branch
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/checkout
+```
+
+```json
+{ "branch": "feature" }
+```
+
+## Tags
+
+### List Tags
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/tags
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "tags": [
+    { "name": "v1.0", "sha": "abc...", "type": "lightweight" },
+    {
+      "name": "v2.0", "sha": "def...", "type": "annotated",
+      "target": "ghi...", "tagger": "Alice", "message": "Release v2.0\n"
+    }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### Create Lightweight Tag
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/tags
+```
+
+```json
+{ "name": "v1.0", "target": "main" }
+```
+
+### Create Annotated Tag
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/tags
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "name": "v2.0",
+  "tagger": "Alice",
+  "email": "alice@example.com",
+  "message": "Release v2.0"
+}
+```
+
+If `message` is present, an annotated tag object is created.
+
+### Delete Tag
+
+```http
+DELETE /api/repos/:owner/:repo/tags/:name
+```
+
+## Diff
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/diff?a=<ref>&b=<ref>
+```
+
+Compares two refs. If `b` is omitted, diffs `a` against its parent commit.
+
+```json
+{
+  "entries": [
+    { "path": "feature.ts", "status": "added", "newSha": "abc..." },
+    { "path": "old.ts", "status": "deleted", "oldSha": "def..." },
+    { "path": "main.ts", "status": "modified", "oldSha": "ghi...", "newSha": "jkl..." }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+## Merge
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/merge
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "target": "main",
+  "source": "feature",
+  "author": "Alice",
+  "email": "alice@example.com",
+  "message": "Merge feature into main"
+}
+```
+
+Returns the merge strategy used:
+
+```json
+{ "sha": "abc...", "strategy": "fast-forward" }
+```
+
+or for a three-way merge:
+
+```json
+{ "sha": "abc...", "strategy": "merge" }
+```
+
+Conflicts are resolved automatically (takes theirs for conflicting files).
+
+## Cherry-Pick
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/cherry-pick
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "commit": "<sha>",
+  "target": "main",
+  "author": "Alice",
+  "email": "alice@example.com"
+}
+```
+
+Applies the changes from the specified commit onto the target branch using three-way merge.
+
+## Revert
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/revert
+```
+
+```json
+{
+  "commit": "<sha>",
+  "target": "main",
+  "author": "Alice",
+  "email": "alice@example.com"
+}
+```
+
+Creates a new commit that undoes the changes introduced by the specified commit.
+
+## Reset
+
+```http
+POST /api/repos/:owner/:repo/reset
+```
+
+```json
+{ "ref": "main", "target": "<sha>" }
+```
+
+Moves the branch ref to point at a different commit.
+
+## Rev-Parse
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/rev-parse?ref=HEAD
+```
+
+Resolves a ref to a commit SHA. Supports: branch names, tag names, `HEAD`, `HEAD~N`, `HEAD^`, `HEAD^2`, raw SHAs.
+
+```json
+{ "sha": "abc..." }
+```
+
+## Show Object
+
+```http
+GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/show?sha=<sha>
+```
+
+Reads a raw git object:
+
+```json
+{ "type": "commit", "size": 234, "content": "tree abc...\nparent ..." }
+```

@@ -0,0 +1,118 @@
+---
+title: WASM Engine
+description: Zig-compiled WASM module for git binary operations.
+---
+
+The core binary operations (SHA-1, zlib, packfiles, deltas) run in a Zig-compiled WASM module with SIMD acceleration. The optimized binary is 791KB after dead-code elimination and size optimization.
+
+## Build Pipeline
+
+```
+Zig 0.15 (ReleaseSmall)
+  → wasm-metadce (remove unused exports)
+  → wasm-opt -Oz (size optimization)
+  → 791KB final binary
+```
+
+Build command:
+
+```bash
+pnpm run build:wasm
+```
+
+## Exports
+
+The WASM module exports 27 functions organized by category:
+
+### Memory Management
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `memory` | Linear memory |
+| `alloc` | Allocate bytes in WASM heap |
+| `resetHeap` | Reset heap (arena allocator) |
+| `getHeapUsed` | Get current heap usage |
+
+### SHA-1
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `sha1_hash` | Hash raw bytes |
+| `sha1_hash_object` | Hash with git object header (e.g., `blob 5\0hello`) |
+
+### Zlib
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `zlib_inflate` | Decompress zlib data |
+| `zlib_inflate_tracked` | Decompress with size tracking |
+| `zlib_deflate` | Compress data |
+
+### Object Parsing
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `parse_object_header` | Parse `type size\0` header |
+| `serialize_object` | Create object with header |
+| `parse_tree` | Parse tree entries |
+
+### Packfile Operations
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `pack_parse_header` | Parse PACK header (magic, version, count) |
+| `pack_parse_entry_header` | Parse entry type and size |
+| `pack_build` | Build a packfile from objects |
+
+### Delta Encoding
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `delta_apply` | Apply delta instructions to base object |
+| `delta_create` | Create delta between two objects |
+
+### Pkt-Line
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `pktline_encode` | Encode data with 4-byte hex length prefix |
+| `pktline_decode` | Decode pkt-line framed data |
+
+### SIMD Utilities
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `simd_memeql` | SIMD-accelerated memory comparison |
+| `simd_memchr` | SIMD-accelerated byte search |
+
+### libgit2 Integration
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `libgit2_init` | Initialize libgit2 |
+| `libgit2_shutdown` | Shut down libgit2 |
+| `libgit2_diff` | Compute diff between trees |
+| `libgit2_revwalk` | Walk commit history |
+| `libgit2_blame` | Line-level blame |
+
+## Size Optimization
+
+The WASM binary was reduced from 1.2MB to 791KB (34% reduction) using:
+
+1. **wasm-metadce** — Dead code elimination based on an export graph. Only 27 exports are kept; all unreachable functions are removed. This reduced function count from ~3000+ to 1272.
+
+2. **wasm-opt -Oz** — Binaryen's aggressive size optimizer. Applies code folding, constant propagation, dead argument elimination, and instruction combining.
+
+Both tools require feature flags for the WASM features used:
+
+```bash
+--enable-simd --enable-bulk-memory --enable-nontrapping-float-to-int
+```
+
+## Zig Source
+
+The WASM source lives in `wasm/` and is built with `zig build wasm -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall`. Key build settings:
+
+- `entry = .disabled` — no `_start` entry point (library mode)
+- `rdynamic = true` — export all public functions (required in Zig 0.15)
+- `stack_size = 4MB` — sufficient for deep tree recursion and large packfiles
